raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that prints summary of new/missing files and which files differ between two directories, sorted alphabetically. Use diff -qr and maybe find. We can use diff -qr folder1 folder2 | sort. That prints differences. For summary, maybe use diff -qr and then parse. But simplest: diff -qr /system/folder1 /system/folder2 | sort. That lists differences. For summary of new/missing: diff -qr already shows "Only in". For differing: "Files ... differ". So command: diff -qr /system/folder1 /system/folder2 | sort.
raw_output_payload.reasoning_content: We need a bash command that prints summary of new/missing files and which files differ between two directories, sorted alphabetically. Use diff -qr and maybe find. We can use diff -qr folder1 folder2 | sort. That prints differences. For summary, maybe use diff -qr and then parse. But simplest: diff -qr /system/folder1 /system/folder2 | sort. That lists differences. For summary of new/missing: diff -qr already shows "Only in". For differing: "Files ... differ". So command: diff -qr /system/folder1 /system/folder2 | sort.
